one_line_description: Canonical SHA256-based identity ensures identical inputs produce identical shards, enabling deterministic replay, deduplication, and audit-grade traceability
source_section: Core Capabilities (Open Core)
proof_pack: Deterministic Shard Identity / Idempotent Ingestion Proof Pack
proof_summary: canonical SHA256 identity is deterministic
equivalent canonical payloads produce the same identity
semantically distinct payloads produce distinct identities
field-order and JSON-order variation do not affect canonical identity
idempotent retry/replay preserves a single authoritative shard/output record
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: System state can be deterministically reconstructed at any point in time from the append-only memory substrate
source_section: Design Principles
proof_pack: Core Proof Summary + Replay/Outbox Durability Proof Pack
proof_summary: outbox replay is deterministic
restart/retry behavior does not lose writes
replay proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: Each memory entry is linked to its causal predecessors, enabling full reconstruction of how system state evolved over time
source_section: Architecture
proof_pack: Core Proof Summary + Replay/Outbox Durability Proof Pack
proof_summary: memory state is replayable from deterministic substrate and outbox behavior
lineage-sensitive processing remains reconstructable through replay artifacts

one_line_description: Memory is never mutated or overwritten; all changes are recorded as new entries, preserving complete historical lineage
source_section: Design Principles
proof_pack: Core Proof Summary + Replay/Outbox Durability Proof Pack
proof_summary: state reconstruction is based on append/replay behavior rather than mutation assumptions
replay/outbox durability protects historical lineage under restart and retry conditions

one_line_description: Duplicate events are silently no-opped; no duplicate shards ever created
source_section: Design Principles
proof_pack: Deterministic Shard Identity / Idempotent Ingestion Proof Pack
proof_summary: duplicate/idempotent ingestion does not create duplicate shards
idempotent retry/replay preserves a single authoritative shard/output record
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: Exactly-once indexing pipeline that guarantees consistent vector state even across failures, restarts, and retries
source_section: Core Capabilities (Open Core)
proof_summary: pending/leased/processed transitions survive retry and replay scenarios
outbox durability is deterministic under restart-style simulation
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: Hard multi-gate tenant enforcement with no silent cross-tenant leakage at any layer
source_section: Design Principles
proof_summary: missing, empty, whitespace, and non-string tenant values fail closed
no aliasing or fallback tenant behavior is allowed
foreign provider results are filtered before they can cross tenant boundaries
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

feature: Authoritative Document Store + Derivative Vector Index
one_line_description: Authoritative document store defines canonical truth while vector index remains a disposable, replayable derivative, ensuring retrieval never corrupts memory integrity
source_section: Architecture
proof_pack: Authoritative Store / Derivative Index Proof Pack
proof_summary: authoritative document write precedes indexing
vector index outage does not corrupt authoritative memory
derivative index can be reconstructed from authoritative records
missing vector hit does not imply missing memory
vector payload divergence does not become canonical truth
duplicate derivative index upsert is idempotent
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: Exponential decay with configurable per-tenant half-life and reinforcement scoring on retrievals
source_section: Core Capabilities (Open Core)
proof_summary: exponential decay reduces unused memory influence deterministically
configurable half-life changes decay behavior predictably
reinforcement increases influence for successful retrievals
reinforcement behavior is deterministic across repeated runs
trust floors/ceilings prevent reinforcement mass alone from making poisoned or low-trust memory authoritative
decay/reinforcement composition is deterministic
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

one_line_description: Commercial extension with Ed25519 signature enforcement, tamper-detectable Evidence Pack export, and policy guardrails
source_section: Governed Mode (Commercial Extension)
proof_pack: Governed Signing / Evidence Pack Integrity Proof Pack
proof_summary: unsigned open-core receipts remain allowed where governed signing is not enforced
governed wrapper signs receipts with Ed25519
verifier accepts valid signed receipts
verifier rejects tampered receipt payloads
verifier rejects signatures from revoked keys
verifier accepts signatures from retired historical keys
Evidence Pack manifest/hash tree is deterministic
Evidence Pack verification fails if any included artifact is modified
proof artifact is byte-identical across double-run

feature: Cortex Proof Spine I1-I7 + Unified Proof Bundle

proof_invariants: I1: deterministic memory / core proof summary
I5: deterministic shard identity / idempotent ingestion
I7: governed signing / Evidence Pack integrity
page_positioning: Use as the primary headline-level Cortex proof claim on public /cortex and Control Cortex surfaces.
feature_delta_summary: unified proof bundle composes I1-I7 proof artifacts
proof_bundle_id equals the deterministic Merkle root
verify-all proof export is available through cortex proof-bundle
proof spine becomes portable, inspectable, and buyer/auditor-facing
stronger temporal and retrieval influence receipts
fact identity and reinforcement semantics tightened
deterministic semantic benchmark artifacts and provider metadata added
Evidence Pack verification and governed key lifecycle proof coverage added

one_line_description: A deterministic proof bundle composes Cortex proof artifacts I1-I7 into a single portable verification artifact for CI, auditors, demos, and downstream Keon Evidence Pack integration.

one_line_description: The proof bundle is identified by its deterministic Merkle root, making the bundle itself content-addressable and tamper-evident.
source_section: proof_bundle_id / merkle_root

one_line_description: `cortex proof-bundle` provides a single verify-all command that emits the full deterministic Cortex proof spine across I1-I7.
source_section: proof-bundle command

one_line_description: Cortex can now produce a portable proof bundle that lets buyers, auditors, and platform teams verify memory correctness claims without trusting marketing copy or runtime screenshots.
source_section: Unified proof bundle
positioning_language: Do not trust the memory layer. Verify it.
commercial_significance: Transforms the Cortex proof spine from internal engineering scaffolding into a buyer-facing and auditor-facing verification artifact.

one_line_description: Cortex should be presented as deterministic AI memory with verifiable proof artifacts, not a vector database or chat-history cache.
source_section: Why Cortex Exists / Proof Spine
approved_copy_points: Vector databases are not memory. They are semantic indexes.
Cortex separates canonical truth from disposable retrieval surfaces.
One command. Seven proof families. Deterministic output. Content-addressed evidence.
Do not trust the memory layer. Verify it.
Signed receipts and deterministic Evidence Packs make memory claims auditable.

Mem0, LangGraph/LangMem, Letta, LlamaIndex, Zep/Graphiti, Cognee, and Hindsight mostly optimize for agents remembering useful context. Cortex optimizes for proving memory correctness under failure, tenancy, replay, indexing drift, trust decay, and governed audit.
Most memory systems optimize recall. Cortex optimizes correctness.
Most memory systems retrieve context. Cortex proves memory lineage.
Most memory systems trust their indexes. Cortex treats indexes as disposable derivatives.
Most memory systems log after the fact. Cortex emits deterministic proof artifacts you can verify.
Verdict: Cortex leads on durability/replay proof. Transactional outbox, leasing, replay proofs, idempotence, and byte-identical artifacts are not the normal pitch in this market.

Verdict: Cortex leads on cryptographic auditability. Ed25519 signing, tamper rejection, revoked key rejection, retired key acceptance, deterministic Evidence Pack manifest/hash tree, and proof bundle composition are not memory features. They are audit infrastructure.
forbidden_copy_points: Do not describe Cortex as only RAG, vector search, or chat history.
Do not imply the vector index is canonical truth.
Do not imply governed signing is required for open-core receipts.
Do not claim runtime execution governance belongs inside Cortex.
page_positioning_delta: Cortex pages should now say the proof spine is I1-I7, not merely planned or aspirational.
Cortex pages may reference deterministic proof and benchmark artifacts as landed engineering outputs.
Governed pages may cite Ed25519 signing, Evidence Pack tamper detection, and key lifecycle proof coverage.
Control surfaces should present receipts, influence receipts, manifests, and artifacts as operator evidence.

source_section: Cortex proof-spine and hardening delta through I7

source_section: Cortex proof-spine and hardening delta through I7

source_section: Cortex proof-spine and hardening delta through I7
one_line_description: Fact identity semantics were tightened so duplicate claims, reinforced facts, and evolving fact slots are distinguishable.
implementation_notes: fact_slot_id stabilizes attribute identity across snapshots
DUPLICATE semantics are separated from REINFORCEMENT semantics
reinforcement can support confidence without collapsing distinct claim lineage
copy_guidance: Do not describe reinforcement as blindly merging facts. Reinforcement strengthens retrieval/trust behavior while preserving lineage semantics.

source_section: Cortex proof-spine and hardening delta through I7
one_line_description: Decay and reinforcement behavior was hardened so memory influence changes predictably without allowing poisoned or low-trust memory to become authoritative by mass alone.
implementation_notes: trust floors/ceilings prevent reinforcement-only promotion of poisoned memory
